interactions.evidence_summary: KRT13 sequesters plakoglobin (γ-catenin), abrogating PG-mediated suppression of c-Myc and elevating MYC expression in cancer cells.
KRT13 knockdown in pancreatic CSCs induces apoptosis, indicating KRT13 suppresses CASP3-mediated apoptotic execution in PDAC.
SOX2 directly binds MYC enhancer and exon regions (ChIP-qPCR confirmed) to restore MYC expression in KRAS-driven PDAC tumor cells.
SOX2 knockdown in PDAC cells markedly induces p27Kip1 (CDKN1B); SOX2 is a ChIP-confirmed direct transcriptional repressor of CDKN1B.
SOX2 knockdown in PDAC induces p21Cip1 (CDKN1A); ChIP confirms SOX2 directly represses CDKN1A to drive G1/S transition.
Dephosphorylated YAP1 translocates to nucleus and binds TEAD1 to drive oncogenic transcription; canonical Hippo effector step downstream of FAT2.
FAT2, as a FAT-family atypical cadherin, signals through the Hippo cascade; FAT-family loss de-represses YAP1 in multiple cancers including pancreatic carcinoma.
FAT2 regulates integrin-PI3K-AKT signaling axis in squamous cancer PDX functional studies, supporting FAT2→PIK3CA activation.
RHCG stabilizes IκB by reducing its phosphorylation, suppressing the IKK complex regulatory subunit IKBKG and blocking NF-κB/p65 nuclear translocation in squamous carcinoma cells.
RHCG overexpression reduces MMP2 expression in cancer cells; RHCG knockdown restores MMP2 levels, directly linking RHCG to MMP2-mediated invasion suppression.
RHCG overexpression promotes apoptosis with increased cleaved CASP3; RHCG siRNA knockdown markedly reduces cleaved Caspase-3 in cancer cells.
IKK complex (IKBKG scaffold) activates NF-κB/p65, which transcriptionally upregulates MMP2; RHCG suppression of IKBKG reduces this MMP2 induction.
TP63 ChIP-seq in PDAC cell lines (BxPC3, SUIT2) shows direct TP63 occupancy at KRT6A enhancers; TP63 knockout abolishes KRT6A expression in squamous-subtype PDA cells.
EFS (CAS-family scaffold) directly activates SRC signaling through c-CRK and RAP1; SRC reciprocally hyperphosphorylates EFS substrate-domain tyrosines, forming a positive feedback loop.

EFS SH3 domain directly contacts FAK (PTK2) polyproline sequences at focal adhesions; integrin activation triggers FAK phosphorylation of EFS, forming a reciprocal scaffold complex essential for PDAC invasion.
Phosphorylated YDxP motifs in the EFS substrate domain directly recruit CRK/CRKL SH2 domains; this CAS-CRK interaction is the canonical output of all CAS-family scaffolds including EFS.
SRC inhibition in pancreatic cancer cell lines (BXPC-3, MIAPaCa-2, PANC-1) suppressed MMP2 activity by ~40%, directly linking SRC to MMP2-driven invasion in PDAC.
FAK autophosphorylation at Y397 recruits PI3K (PIK3CA p85/p110) in PDAC cells, directly linking the EFS-FAK focal adhesion complex to AKT1 survival signaling.
EFS/CAS-family scaffold integrates integrin signals that recruit and activate SHP2 (PTPN11); PTPN11 deletion profoundly inhibits mutant KRAS-driven PDAC tumor development in murine models.
Single-cell RNA-seq of PDA patient tumors shows TP63 and SOX2 are co-expressed in ductal epithelial cells; TP63-high tumors exhibit significantly elevated SOX2 in the squamous PDAC compartment.
SHP2 (PTPN11) is required for full RAS-ERK cascade activation; dominant-negative PTPN11 blocks RAS/ERK signaling, placing PTPN11 directly upstream of RAF1 in KRAS-driven PDAC.

CAS-family scaffolds (including EFS) assemble integrin-FAK complexes that activate PI3K/AKT1 to suppress anoikis; CAS protein loss disrupts focal adhesion integrity and impairs AKT-dependent cancer cell survival.
CRK recruits DOCK180 to activate RAC1, which feeds into PAK1-MEK-ERK (MAPK1) cascade; CRK overexpression in pancreatic and other solid tumors drives ERK-dependent proliferation and invasion.
Phosphorylated EFS substrate-domain YxxP motifs recruit SH2-domain adaptors including GRB2, which bridges CAS-family scaffolds to SOS1-KRAS activation in cancer focal adhesion complexes.

Phosphorylated EFS substrate-domain YxxP motifs recruit GRB2, which directly complexes with SOS1 to catalyze KRAS GTP-loading; this CAS-GRB2-SOS1 axis is conserved across integrin-activated cancer focal adhesion complexes.

CAS-family scaffolds (including EFS) potentiate EGFR-dependent signaling at focal adhesions; NSP proteins anchor CAS proteins near plasma membrane RTKs, amplifying EGFR phosphorylation in cancer cells.
EFS dephosphorylation in T-cell signaling releases PLCG1, which cleaves PIP2 to generate DAG, directly activating PKC (PRKCA); this EFS-PLCG1-PRKCA axis is conserved in epithelial cancer contexts.
EFS scaffolds SRC at focal adhesions; SRC directly phosphorylates KRAS Tyr32/Tyr64 in pancreatic cancer cells, modulating KRAS GTP-loading and downstream effector engagement.

EFS co-expression with squamous DEGs (KRT6A, KRT13) in basal-like PDAC suggests EFS focal-adhesion signaling supports the TP63-driven squamous transcriptional program; TP63 drives KRT6A enhancers in PDA cells.

EFS focal-adhesion scaffolding promotes integrin-dependent cell spreading and migration; SEMA3A is a repulsive axon-guidance cue that collapses integrin-mediated adhesion complexes, placing EFS and SEMA3A in opposing regulatory roles in PDAC invasion.
PKC activation drives clathrin-dependent OATP1A2 internalization, reducing surface expression and transport function in vitro (PMID:21133891).
PKC phosphorylates RAF1 S338 and facilitates RAS-GTP-RAF complex formation, coupling PKC activity to KRAS effector signaling in cancer cells.
SLCO1A2-mediated intracellular bile acid accumulation activates bile acid receptor signaling linked to MMP2 upregulation in GI/pancreatic cancer contexts.
LINC02253 recruits m6A writer METTL3 to KRT18 mRNA, stabilizing it and driving oncogenic MAPK/ERK activation in GC cells.
METTL3 promotes PDAC progression via m6A-stabilized DDX23, activating PI3K/Akt signaling confirmed by rescue experiments in pancreatic cancer cells.
METTL3-stabilized KRT18 mRNA activates MAPK/ERK (ERK1/2) signaling, confirmed by rescue experiments in cancer cells.
KRT18 stabilized by LINC02253-recruited METTL3 activates MAPK/ERK including ERK1 (MAPK3) to promote tumor growth and invasion.
In PDAC, METTL3-mediated m6A stabilization of ID2 mRNA activates PI3K/AKT signaling to upregulate SOX2 stemness factor.
LINC02253 regulates keratin family mRNA stability via METTL3; KRT13 is a co-expressed squamous keratin DEG in PDAC malignant cells.

AC009271.1: placeholder lncRNA, no HGNC-approved functional symbol, no published mechanistic characterization

experiments.description: Verified LLM edges plus curated pathway priors.
experiments.name: llm_plus_priors

experiments.description: Verified LLM edges, curated pathway priors, and pre-simulation DepMap pruning.
experiments.name: llm_plus_priors_pruned

candidates.benchmark_assessment: Weak: mean effect −0.056, no DepMap hit.
candidates.rationale: EFS activates SRC→KRAS/RAF1/BRAF/PIK3CA/AKT1 via scaffold signaling. Solver confirms all 9 pathway nodes turn off. Highly upregulated DEG (log2FC 26.4). Weak DepMap support tempers confidence.

candidates.benchmark_assessment: No SOX2 benchmark row; EFS weak.
candidates.rationale: Adds SOX2 KO to block MYC activation and relieve CDKN1A/CDKN1B repression, reinforcing cell-cycle arrest beyond EFS-mediated pathway shutdown. SOX2 is top-3 DEG (log2FC 28.7).

candidates.benchmark_assessment: No TP63 benchmark row; EFS weak.
candidates.rationale: TP63 drives squamous-subtype program (KRT6A) and activates SOX2. Combined with EFS KO, disrupts both KRAS effector cascade and squamous differentiation axis sustaining PDAC.

candidates.benchmark_assessment: No benchmark data available for SOX2.
candidates.rationale: SOX2 directly activates MYC and represses CDKN1A/CDKN1B in PDAC. Knockout restores cell-cycle inhibitors. However, no direct graph edges to core KRAS pathway nodes limit pathway shutdown breadth.

candidates.benchmark_assessment: No benchmark for SOX2/TP63; EFS weak.
candidates.rationale: Triple KO maximizes coverage: EFS shuts KRAS cascade, SOX2 blocks MYC/cell-cycle, TP63 collapses squamous program. Three-gene cost and no benchmark support reduce practical confidence.

llm_knockout.recommendation_summary: EFS knockout is the top mechanistic choice: it feeds KRAS, RAF1, BRAF, MAP2K1, PIK3CA, and AKT1 via SRC scaffolding, shutting down the full KRAS signaling cascade in one intervention. However, DepMap benchmark support is weak (mean gene effect −0.056, no hit), so experimental validation is essential. Two-gene combinations with SOX2 or TP63 offer marginal added coverage.

meta.title: From Differential Expression to Virtual Knockouts
